evals.prompt: Can you check my screenshots in src/eap/building-apps/data/fetch-data/aggregate.md before I push? I want to make sure they follow the design rules.
evals.expected_output: Skill resolves every PNG referenced by the markdown, reads each one, scores against visual-rules-screenshots.md, and returns a short issues-only summary. If there's no rubric yet it should stop with the calibration instruction.

Skill runs in branch-diff mode (no argument). It calls `git diff master...HEAD -- '*.png'`, resolves each changed PNG to its referencing article, and reports. Empty changeset should produce the 'no screenshots to validate' message.
Skill single-image flow. Resolves the PNG, finds the article that references it, reads the Figma URL from its frontmatter, and reviews against the rubric. Output is one image section only.

evals.prompt: are these screenshots good to go?
evals.expected_output: Vague invocation with no argument should still trigger and fall back to branch-diff. If no changed PNGs are found, skill should say so clearly rather than scanning the whole repo.
